title: Agent Instructions
text: Use committed docs durable project knowledge. Keep raw planning notes, temporary context, generated scratch work _scratch/. commit _scratch/, .fp/, secrets, dependencies, build outputs, local caches.

title: Agent Instructions
text: project uses pre-commit framework. config (.pre-commit-config.yaml) cloned repo; clone enables hooks : pre-commit Python tool. non-Python templates, install uv tool install pre-commit pipx install pre-commit.
code: pre-commit install && pre-commit install --hook-type pre-push

title: Agent Instructions
text: every commit, lightweight hooks run: end--file fixer, trailing-whitespace trimming, merge-conflict detection, YAML/TOML validation, mixed-line-ending case-conflict guards, check-added-large-files — portable 5 MB size guard blocks accidentally committing heavy blobs (big blob bloats .git history even deletion).

title: Agent Instructions
text: git push, verify hook runs project’s verify command — chain CI runs. Server-side branch protection unavailable GitHub plan, local pre-push gate stand-branch protection: blocks push whose tree turn CI red. @FP_AGENTS.md

title: Changelog
text: notable changes project documented .

title: Contributing

title: NA
text: Use fp issue task tracking - use built-todo tools Create subissues --parent flag - never use markdown checklists (- [ ]) Break work atomic tasks (1-3 hours )

title: NA
text: fp issue list --status todo - find available work fp issue update --status -progress <id> - claim starting fp comment <id> "progress..." - log every milestone fp issue update --status done <id> - mark complete finished commit work, mention fp issue message, unless otherwise instructed.

title: NA
text: Run fp comment <id> "..." every milestone Write comments significant commits Always leave final comment ending session

title: NA
code: fp tree [parent-id]        # View issue hierarchy (optionally only show tree of parent-id) fp issue list --status X   # Filter by status (todo/in-progress/done) fp search <query>          # Search issues (AND by default, OR, "phrases", -negation) fp issue create --title "..." --parent X --property key=value fp issue update --status X <id> --property key=value fp comment <id> "message" fp comment update <comment-id> "new message"  # Alias: edit fp comment delete <comment-id>                # Soft-delete/hide a comment fp context <id>            # Load full issue context fp guide extension         # Print the extensions authoring guide fp guide brainstorm        # Print the brainstorm-authoring guide

title: NA

title: Introduction to pslr
text: Public Suffix List (PSL) community-curated list domain suffixes Internet users can directly register names. pslr bundles pinned snapshot list implements official prevailing-rule algorithm answer two core questions hostname: Public suffix (also called effective top-level domain, eTLD): suffix registrations happen, e.g. co.uk example.co.uk. Registrable domain (eTLD+1): public suffix plus one label left registrant actually controls, e.g. example.co.uk. matcher compiled cpp11 needs external system library. Hostname canonicalization (case folding Unicode/IDNA handling) delegated punycoder package.
code: public_suffix("www.example.co.uk") #> [1] "co.uk" registrable_domain("www.example.co.uk") #> [1] "example.co.uk"

title: Introduction to pslr
text: Rule — line list, com, *.ck, !www.ck. Normal rule — literal suffix (com, co.uk). Wildcard rule — *.ck means every label directly ck public suffix. Exception rule — !www.ck carves single name back wildcard. Default rule — spec’s implicit *: unlisted TLD label treated public suffix. Section — list split ICANN part (official domain hierarchy) PRIVATE part (suffixes operated companies, e.g. github.io). prevailing rule chosen : exception beats wildcard, longest match beats shorter matches, implicit default applies nothing else .
code: public_suffix("a.b.kobe.jp") # a wildcard match under kobe.jp #> [1] "b.kobe.jp" public_suffix("city.kobe.jp") # an exception match under kobe.jp #> [1] "kobe.jp"

title: Introduction to pslr
text: section selects rules eligible. Filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, asking one section never silently borrows rule .
code: # github.io is a PRIVATE rule sitting under the ICANN suffix io. public_suffix("user.github.io", section = "all") # default scope, both sections #> [1] "github.io" public_suffix("user.github.io", section = "icann") # the ICANN rule for io #> [1] "io" public_suffix("user.github.io", section = "private") #> [1] "github.io"

title: Introduction to pslr
text: restrict section host matches explicit rule , query falls implicit default rule rather failing. plain ICANN host queried section = "private" therefore resolves last label via default rule: distinguish “explicit rule matched” real match, combine section unknown = "na" ().
code: public_suffix("example.com", section = "private") #> [1] "com"

title: Introduction to pslr
text: default unlisted suffix handled implicit * rule, made-TLD still yields public suffix. Pass unknown = "na" require explicit rule get NA otherwise.
code: public_suffix("example.madeuptld") # default rule #> [1] "madeuptld" public_suffix("example.madeuptld", unknown = "na") # explicit-only #> [1] NA

title: Introduction to pslr
text: is_public_suffix() reports whether host public suffix. default policy unlisted single label TRUE via implicit rule; use unknown = "na" test explicit list membership instead.
code: is_public_suffix("co.uk") #> [1] TRUE is_public_suffix("madeuptld") # TRUE via the implicit default rule #> [1] TRUE is_public_suffix("madeuptld", unknown = "na") # explicit membership only #> [1] NA

title: Introduction to pslr
text: Input may ASCII, Unicode, -label (xn--) hostnames; equivalent spellings canonicalize answer. Output ASCII -labels default; pass output = "unicode" decode .
code: public_suffix("example.рф") # ASCII A-label by default #> [1] "xn--p1ai" public_suffix("example.рф", output = "unicode") # decoded to Unicode #> [1] "рф" public_suffix("example.xn--p1ai") # the A-label spelling agrees #> [1] "xn--p1ai"

title: Introduction to pslr
text: single terminal root dot preserved hostname-shaped output, fully-qualified name round-trips:
code: public_suffix("www.example.com.") #> [1] "com." registrable_domain("www.example.com.") #> [1] "example.com."

title: Introduction to pslr
text: suffix_extract() splits host subdomain, registrant label, suffix; public_suffix_rule() reports rule prevailed, useful auditing. query functions vectorised, length- name-preserving, NA-safe. Invalid input (URLs, IPv6, empty labels, dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, …) NA default; pass invalid = "error" abort first invalid element.
code: suffix_extract("blog.user.github.io") #>                 input                host subdomain domain    suffix #> 1 blog.user.github.io blog.user.github.io      blog   user github.io #>   registrable_domain #> 1     user.github.io public_suffix_rule(c("www.ck", "a.b.kobe.jp", "example.madeuptld")) #>               input        host_ascii      rule      kind rule_section #> 1            www.ck            www.ck   !www.ck exception        icann #> 2       a.b.kobe.jp       a.b.kobe.jp *.kobe.jp  wildcard        icann #> 3 example.madeuptld example.madeuptld         *   default         <NA> #>   public_suffix_ascii #> 1                  ck #> 2           b.kobe.jp #> 3           madeuptld

title: Introduction to pslr
text: package ships pinned snapshot, works fully offline bundled list default every query. psl_refresh() function touches network: explicit, HTTPS-, validated download user cache. psl_use() chooses list backs session. Activation session-validated state changes; failed refresh never replaces working cache active list.
code: # Download and validate a fresh list into the user cache, then activate it: psl_refresh(activate = TRUE)  # Switch the active list for this session: psl_use("cache") # the latest refreshed snapshot psl_use("bundled") # back to the shipped snapshot psl_use("path", path = "my_list.dat") # a custom file

title: Introduction to pslr
text: public-suffix result depends list answered hosts normalized. psl_version() reports — source-snapshot provenance runtime normalization identifiers — result can reproduced later. Record row alongside reproducibility-sensitive output. psl_rules() exposes active rule table : shipped index generated different normalization profile Unicode version installed punycoder, list transparently rebuilt memory source activation, index never mixed hosts normalized different profile.
code: psl_version() #>    source path            retrieved_at            list_date #> 1 bundled <NA> 2026-06-15 16:18:34 UTC 2026-06-13T21:47:08Z #>                                     commit   size #> 1 9186eeeda85cef35b1551d00731464939c765cab 332703 #>                                                                  checksum #> 1 sha256:54fb5c65a1e21aad963acd74a204370b5f517071e8b8e140c48de40727f0171c #>   normalizer normalizer_version         normalization_profile unicode_version #> 1  punycoder              1.1.0 uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1          16.0.0 nrow(psl_rules("icann")) #> [1] 6933 head(psl_rules("private"), 3) #>      rule canonical_rule   kind section labels #> 1  co.krd         co.krd normal private      2 #> 2 edu.krd        edu.krd normal private      2 #> 3  art.pl         art.pl normal private      2

title: Introduction to pslr
text: Hostnames, URLs. query functions accept DNS hostnames. URL-shaped input rejected invalid; parse host URL first. Explicit network . Nothing package load, queries, examples, tests touches network. psl_refresh() , call . HTTPS-, rejects embedded credentials downgrade redirects, enforces source-size ceiling. PSL advisory. best-effort community list, authoritative statement ownership security boundary . Treat registrable-domain result heuristic grouping, proof control. Session-global active list. active list per-session global state; per-call list switching. Concurrent per-list queries scope release.

title: Introduction to pslr
text: pslr part small ecosystem R packages author: punycoder — Punycode IDNA codec pslr uses host canonicalization. Use directly raw Unicode ↔︎ ACE round-trips outside PSL context. rurl — full URL parsing, normalization, cleaning, joining toolkit uses pslr PSL engine. Reach need work complete URLs rather bare hostnames.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bart Turczynski. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Turczynski B (2026). pslr: Public Suffix List Engine. R package version 1.0.2, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=pslr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {{pslr}: Public Suffix List Engine},   author = {Bart Turczynski},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.2},   url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=pslr}, }

title: Public Suffix List Engine
text: focused, spec-complete implementation Public Suffix List (PSL) R. pslr bundles reproducible, pinned PSL snapshot implements official prevailing-rule algorithm answer public-suffix (eTLD) registrable-domain (eTLD+1) queries. Distinguishes ICANN PRIVATE rule sections. Accepts Unicode, ASCII, -label hostnames via punycoder canonicalization; returns ASCII Unicode output. Works fully offline bundled snapshot; explicit, validated psl_refresh() network path. Matcher compiled cpp11; external system library required.

title: Public Suffix List Engine
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub: pslr depends punycoder, installed automatically CRAN.
code: install.packages("pslr") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("bart-turczynski/pslr")

title: Public Suffix List Engine
text: See vignette("introduction", package = "pslr") full tour: section choice, unknown-suffix policy, IDN output, terminal dots, refresh activation, reproducibility, security notes.
code: library(pslr)  public_suffix("www.example.co.uk") #> [1] "co.uk" registrable_domain("www.example.co.uk") #> [1] "example.co.uk"  # ICANN vs PRIVATE sections public_suffix("user.github.io") #> [1] "github.io" public_suffix("user.github.io", section = "icann") #> [1] "io"  # Explicit membership vs the implicit default rule is_public_suffix("madeuptld")                 # implicit "*" #> [1] TRUE is_public_suffix("madeuptld", unknown = "na") # explicit only #> [1] NA  # Split a host, or inspect the prevailing rule suffix_extract("blog.user.github.io") #>                 input                host subdomain domain    suffix #> 1 blog.user.github.io blog.user.github.io      blog   user github.io #>   registrable_domain #> 1     user.github.io public_suffix_rule("a.b.kobe.jp") #>         input  host_ascii      rule     kind rule_section public_suffix_ascii #> 1 a.b.kobe.jp a.b.kobe.jp *.kobe.jp wildcard        icann           b.kobe.jp

title: Public Suffix List Engine
text: result depends list answered hosts normalized. psl_version() reports active-list provenance plus runtime normalization identifiers; record alongside reproducibility-sensitive output.

title: Public Suffix List Engine
text: pslr part small ecosystem R packages author: punycoder — Punycode IDNA codec pslr uses host canonicalization PSL matching. Use directly raw Unicode ↔︎ ACE round-trips. rurl — full URL parsing, normalization, cleaning, joining toolkit. Uses pslr PSL engine; reach need domain extraction.

what: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
text: TRUE exactly valid canonical host equals public suffix selected policy. Returns NA whenever public_suffix() return NA (missing invalid input, unresolved host unknown = "na"). default unknown = "default", unlisted single label "madeuptld" TRUE via implicit * rule; ask unknown = "na" test explicit membership instead.

title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
code: is_public_suffix(   domain,   section = c("all", "icann", "private"),   unknown = c("default", "na"),   invalid = c("na", "error") )

title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
text: domain Character vector DNS hostnames (URLs). element may mixed-case ASCII, Unicode, -label hostname, single label, hostname exactly one terminal root dot. See Input contract. section rule sections eligible: "" (default; ICANN PRIVATE), "icann", "private". Section filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, "private" silently add ICANN rules; host matching rule section falls implicit default rule unless unknown = "na". unknown "default" (default) applies spec's implicit * rule, unlisted single label public suffix; "na" returns NA explicit rule selected section matches. invalid "na" (default) returns NA invalid element without warning; "error" aborts first invalid element, reporting 1-based index.

title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
text: logical vector length(domain), preserving names domain.

title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
text: NA treated missing (returns NA), invalid. Invalid elements include empty whitespace-strings, leading consecutive dots, URL syntax, IPv6 addresses, canonical dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, labels fail hostname/IDNA validation. Wrong argument types non-scalar unknown option values always abort regardless invalid.

title: Is a host itself a public suffix? — is_public_suffix
code: is_public_suffix("com") #> [1] TRUE is_public_suffix("example.com") #> [1] FALSE is_public_suffix("madeuptld") #> [1] TRUE is_public_suffix("madeuptld", unknown = "na") #> [1] NA

what: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
text: Downloads, validates, publishes fresh Public Suffix List user cache. function package accesses network, call explicitly.

title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
code: psl_refresh(   url = "https://publicsuffix.org/list/public_suffix_list.dat",   force = FALSE,   activate = FALSE )

title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
text: url Absolute https URL list source. Defaults official list. URLs another scheme embedded credentials rejected, redirect non-HTTPS URL refused. force FALSE (default), successfully validated cache younger 24 hours reused without download, respecting upstream download guidance. TRUE forces fresh download. activate TRUE, resulting snapshot becomes active list session, exactly psl_use() activate . FALSE (default), cache updated active list unchanged.

title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
text: Invisibly, one-row data.frame shaped like psl_version() describing selected cache snapshot, whether activated.

title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
text: Cache age measured successful network retrieval timestamp; reusing fresh cache advance timestamp. download goes temporary file binary mode must larger documented maximum (16 MiB). source fully validated – UTF-8, section markers, rule grammar, conflicting rules, successful canonicalization every rule – exact -section duplicates warn deduplicated. Source metadata published validation succeeds, using atomic commit never exposes partial mismatched snapshot. failed refresh never replaces valid cache active matcher.

title: Refresh the cached Public Suffix List from upstream — psl_refresh
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ psl_refresh() psl_refresh(force = TRUE, activate = TRUE) } # }

what: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
title: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
text: Returns explicit rules active list base data.frame, one row per rule. implicit default * rule included.

title: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
code: psl_rules(section = c("all", "icann", "private"))

title: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
text: section rule sections return: "" (default), "icann", "private".

title: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
text: base data.frame columns, order: rule (original source rule text), canonical_rule (canonicalized rule, including *. ! marker), kind ("normal", "wildcard", "exception"), section ("icann" "private"), labels (integer rule depth, counting wildcard label). Rows ordered first section (ICANN PRIVATE) source-file order.

title: Rules of the active Public Suffix List — psl_rules
code: head(psl_rules("icann")) #>     rule canonical_rule   kind section labels #> 1     ac             ac normal   icann      1 #> 2 com.ac         com.ac normal   icann      2 #> 3 edu.ac         edu.ac normal   icann      2 #> 4 gov.ac         gov.ac normal   icann      2 #> 5 mil.ac         mil.ac normal   icann      2 #> 6 net.ac         net.ac normal   icann      2 nrow(psl_rules("private")) #> [1] 3279

what: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
text: Switches list backing every query current R session. change session-validated session state changes; failure leaves previously active list usable. successful switch invalidates match-result cache.

title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
code: psl_use(source = c("bundled", "cache", "path"), path = NULL)

title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
text: source load list : "bundled" (pinned package snapshot), "cache" (latest successfully validated snapshot psl_refresh()), "path" (custom file). path source = "path", single readable PSL-format UTF-8 file containing one complete ICANN section one complete PRIVATE section, using official markers. Must NULL source.

title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
text: Invisibly, psl_version() row newly active list.

title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
text: custom path held runtime duplicate policy psl_refresh(): exact -section duplicates warn deduplicated, conflicting rule kinds labels fatal. Cache custom-path sources read source form indexed runtime normalizer; never reuse bundled generated index.

title: Choose the active Public Suffix List for this session — psl_use
code: psl_use("bundled") if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ psl_use("cache") psl_use("path", path = "my_list.dat") } # }

what: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
title: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
text: Returns one-row data.frame describing list currently active R session: source-snapshot provenance normalization identifiers actually used index active matcher. Reproducing query result requires active-list identity normalization identifiers (PRD s10), reproducibility-sensitive workflow record row.

title: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
code: psl_version()

title: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
text: one-row base data.frame columns described Details.

title: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
text: columns, order, : source "bundled", "cache", "path". path File path "cache" "path" source; NA otherwise. retrieved_at Network retrieval timestamp, NA. list_date Upstream list date, NA unknown. commit Upstream commit SHA, NA unknown. size Source byte size (integer). checksum Source checksum, including algorithm prefix (e.g. "sha256:..."). normalizer dependency providing canonicalization, currently "punycoder". normalizer_version installed package version. normalization_profile stable case-mapping / IDNA / validation profile identifier. unicode_version Unicode data version used profile. Unavailable metadata typed NA, never omitted. normalization identifiers describe implementation used current session, whether active list came bundled snapshot, user cache, custom path; -memory compatibility rebuild (PRD s8.3) updates without altering shipped source identity checksum.

title: Identity of the active Public Suffix List — psl_version
code: psl_version() #>    source path            retrieved_at            list_date #> 1 bundled <NA> 2026-06-15 16:18:34 UTC 2026-06-13T21:47:08Z #>                                     commit   size #> 1 9186eeeda85cef35b1551d00731464939c765cab 332703 #>                                                                  checksum #> 1 sha256:54fb5c65a1e21aad963acd74a204370b5f517071e8b8e140c48de40727f0171c #>   normalizer normalizer_version         normalization_profile unicode_version #> 1  punycoder              1.1.0 uts46-nontransitional-std3-v1          16.0.0

what: pslr: Public Suffix List Engine — pslr-package
title: pslr: Public Suffix List Engine — pslr-package
text: focused implementation Public Suffix List (PSL). Bundles reproducible, pinned PSL snapshot implements official prevailing-rule algorithm answer public-suffix (eTLD) registrable-domain (eTLD+1) queries. Distinguishes ICANN PRIVATE rule sections, accepts Unicode ASCII hostnames via 'punycoder' canonicalization, supports explicit, validated offline refresh path. matcher compiled 'cpp11' requires external system library. Used PSL engine 'rurl' package.

title: pslr: Public Suffix List Engine — pslr-package
text: Maintainer: Bart Turczynski bartek@turczynski.pl (ORCID) Authors: Bart Turczynski bartek@turczynski.pl (ORCID)

what: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
text: Returns public suffix (effective top-level domain, eTLD) host selected Public Suffix List policy, following official prevailing-rule algorithm.

title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
code: public_suffix(   domain,   section = c("all", "icann", "private"),   output = c("ascii", "unicode"),   unknown = c("default", "na"),   invalid = c("na", "error") )

title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
text: domain Character vector DNS hostnames (URLs). element may mixed-case ASCII, Unicode, -label hostname, single label, hostname exactly one terminal root dot. See Input contract. section rule sections eligible: "" (default; ICANN PRIVATE), "icann", "private". Section filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, "private" silently add ICANN rules; host matching rule section falls implicit default rule unless unknown = "na". output "ascii" (default) returns lowercase -labels; "unicode" decodes matching. terminal root dot preserved either way. unknown "default" (default) applies spec's implicit * rule, unlisted single label public suffix; "na" returns NA explicit rule selected section matches. invalid "na" (default) returns NA invalid element without warning; "error" aborts first invalid element, reporting 1-based index.

title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
text: character vector length(domain), preserving names domain. attributes dropped.

title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
text: NA treated missing (returns NA), invalid. Invalid elements include empty whitespace-strings, leading consecutive dots, URL syntax, IPv6 addresses, canonical dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, labels fail hostname/IDNA validation. Wrong argument types non-scalar unknown option values always abort regardless invalid.

title: Public suffix of a host — public_suffix
code: public_suffix("www.example.com") #> [1] "com" public_suffix("example.co.uk") #> [1] "co.uk" public_suffix("example.com.") #> [1] "com." public_suffix("madeuptld", unknown = "na") #> [1] NA

what: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
text: Inspect prevailing PSL rule host

title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
code: public_suffix_rule(   domain,   section = c("all", "icann", "private"),   unknown = c("default", "na"),   invalid = c("na", "error") )

title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
text: domain Character vector DNS hostnames (URLs). element may mixed-case ASCII, Unicode, -label hostname, single label, hostname exactly one terminal root dot. See Input contract. section rule sections eligible: "" (default; ICANN PRIVATE), "icann", "private". Section filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, "private" silently add ICANN rules; host matching rule section falls implicit default rule unless unknown = "na". unknown "default" (default) applies spec's implicit * rule, unlisted single label public suffix; "na" returns NA explicit rule selected section matches. invalid "na" (default) returns NA invalid element without warning; "error" aborts first invalid element, reporting 1-based index.

title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
text: base data.frame one row per input columns, order: input (original), host_ascii (canonical -label host), rule (canonical rule including *. !, "*" implicit default), kind ("normal", "wildcard", "exception", "default"), rule_section ("icann", "private", NA default/result), public_suffix_ascii (derived -label public suffix). Invalid rows NA every derived column. valid host left unresolved unknown = "na" keeps host_ascii rule suffix columns NA. exception rule retains ! auditability. Zero-length input returns zero-row frame; -invalid input keeps one row per input.

title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
text: NA treated missing (returns NA), invalid. Invalid elements include empty whitespace-strings, leading consecutive dots, URL syntax, IPv6 addresses, canonical dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, labels fail hostname/IDNA validation. Wrong argument types non-scalar unknown option values always abort regardless invalid.

title: Inspect the prevailing PSL rule for each host — public_suffix_rule
code: public_suffix_rule("www.example.co.uk") #>               input        host_ascii  rule   kind rule_section #> 1 www.example.co.uk www.example.co.uk co.uk normal        icann #>   public_suffix_ascii #> 1               co.uk public_suffix_rule("madeuptld") #>       input host_ascii rule    kind rule_section public_suffix_ascii #> 1 madeuptld  madeuptld    * default         <NA>           madeuptld

what: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
text: Returns registrable domain (eTLD+1) host: public suffix plus one host label left. NA label exists (host public suffix) public suffix NA.

title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
code: registrable_domain(   domain,   section = c("all", "icann", "private"),   output = c("ascii", "unicode"),   unknown = c("default", "na"),   invalid = c("na", "error") )

title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
text: domain Character vector DNS hostnames (URLs). element may mixed-case ASCII, Unicode, -label hostname, single label, hostname exactly one terminal root dot. See Input contract. section rule sections eligible: "" (default; ICANN PRIVATE), "icann", "private". Section filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, "private" silently add ICANN rules; host matching rule section falls implicit default rule unless unknown = "na". output "ascii" (default) returns lowercase -labels; "unicode" decodes matching. terminal root dot preserved either way. unknown "default" (default) applies spec's implicit * rule, unlisted single label public suffix; "na" returns NA explicit rule selected section matches. invalid "na" (default) returns NA invalid element without warning; "error" aborts first invalid element, reporting 1-based index.

title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
text: character vector length(domain), preserving names domain. attributes dropped.

title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
text: NA treated missing (returns NA), invalid. Invalid elements include empty whitespace-strings, leading consecutive dots, URL syntax, IPv6 addresses, canonical dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, labels fail hostname/IDNA validation. Wrong argument types non-scalar unknown option values always abort regardless invalid.

title: Registrable domain of a host — registrable_domain
code: registrable_domain("www.example.co.uk") #> [1] "example.co.uk" registrable_domain("com") #> [1] NA registrable_domain("foo.madeuptld", unknown = "na") #> [1] NA

title: Split hosts into subdomain, registrant label, and public suffix — suffix_extract

title: Split hosts into subdomain, registrant label, and public suffix — suffix_extract
text: domain Character vector DNS hostnames (URLs). element may mixed-case ASCII, Unicode, -label hostname, single label, hostname exactly one terminal root dot. See Input contract. section rule sections eligible: "" (default; ICANN PRIVATE), "icann", "private". Section filtering happens prevailing-rule selection, "private" silently add ICANN rules; host matching rule section falls implicit default rule unless unknown = "na". output "ascii" (default) returns lowercase -labels; "unicode" decodes matching. terminal root dot preserved either way. unknown "default" (default) applies spec's implicit * rule, unlisted single label public suffix; "na" returns NA explicit rule selected section matches. invalid "na" (default) returns NA invalid element without warning; "error" aborts first invalid element, reporting 1-based index.

title: Split hosts into subdomain, registrant label, and public suffix — suffix_extract
text: base data.frame one row per input columns, order: input (original, unchanged), host (canonical host output form), subdomain (labels left registrable domain; "" none), domain (single registrant label left suffix), suffix (public suffix), registrable_domain (eTLD+1). domain, subdomain, registrable_domain NA host public suffix. public-suffix resolution NA, every derived column except input successfully normalized host NA. Zero-length input returns zero-row frame; -invalid input keeps one row per input. Root dots preserved host, suffix, registrable_domain .

title: Split hosts into subdomain, registrant label, and public suffix — suffix_extract
text: NA treated missing (returns NA), invalid. Invalid elements include empty whitespace-strings, leading consecutive dots, URL syntax, IPv6 addresses, canonical dotted-decimal IPv4 literals, labels fail hostname/IDNA validation. Wrong argument types non-scalar unknown option values always abort regardless invalid.

title: Split hosts into subdomain, registrant label, and public suffix — suffix_extract
code: suffix_extract("www.example.co.uk") #>               input              host subdomain  domain suffix #> 1 www.example.co.uk www.example.co.uk       www example  co.uk #>   registrable_domain #> 1      example.co.uk suffix_extract(c("example.com", "com", NA)) #>         input        host subdomain  domain suffix registrable_domain #> 1 example.com example.com           example    com        example.com #> 2         com         com      <NA>    <NA>    com               <NA> #> 3        <NA>        <NA>      <NA>    <NA>   <NA>               <NA>

title: pslr 1.0.2
text: Dropped redundant strict = TRUE argument punycoder::host_normalize() calls. punycoder removed inert strict flag favour explicit UTS #46 flags (defaulting strict profile), bare call behavior-preserving forward-compatible release. user-visible change; keeps pslr installable upcoming punycoder release. Refactored psl_canonicalize(), parse_psl_lines(), core matcher resolution, psl_refresh()/psl_use() smaller helpers clear goodpractice cyclomatic-complexity function-length findings. Pure internal restructuring; behavior API change.

title: pslr 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-22 Launch-readiness audit follow-ups (API changes). suffix_extract(output = "unicode") longer turns empty subdomain NA. absent subdomain reported "" "ascii" "unicode" output, matching documented schema. Choice-style option arguments (section, output, unknown, invalid, psl_use()’s source) now abort caller supplies non-scalar value, even one happens equal formal’s default vector (e.g. invalid = c("na", "error")). Previously call mistaken untouched default silently used first choice. Omitted options still default first choice. corrupt cache marker (current.rds) now handled gracefully instead leaking raw readRDS() “unknown input format” error. psl_refresh(force = TRUE) ignores unreadable marker republishes valid cache, psl_use("cache") reports pslr cache-corruption error remediation. PSL sources repeated ICANN PRIVATE section now rejected. official format carries exactly one complete section ; second BEGIN either aborts parse instead loading copies. zero-length non-character domain (e.g. numeric(0), NULL) now aborts documented type error instead silently coerced empty result. consistent input contract: wrong argument type programming error regardless length. valid empty character vector character(0) still returns zero-length result.

title: pslr 1.0.0
text: First public release: spec-complete Public Suffix List engine R. Bundled Public Suffix List snapshot pinned upstream commit 9186eee (list date 2026-06-13), deterministic data-raw/ regeneration script, internal validated rule index, generation metadata (commit, source URL, checksum, normalization profile, Unicode version), MPL-2.0 data licensing separate package’s MIT code license. Added public query API: public_suffix(), registrable_domain(), is_public_suffix(), suffix_extract(), public_suffix_rule(). vectorised, length- name-preserving, NA-safe, share section / output / unknown / invalid policies. Input canonicalized punycoder terminal-dot preservation dotted-decimal IPv4 literal rejection, repeated queries served bounded session cache keyed host, active-list identity, section. Added refresh activation: psl_refresh(), psl_use(), psl_version(), psl_rules(). psl_refresh() network path – explicit, https-, credential- downgrade-redirect-rejecting download size ceiling, full validation, 24-hour reuse throttle, atomic cache publish never exposes partial snapshot replaces valid cache failed refresh. psl_use() switches session’s active list bundled snapshot, user cache, custom path, validating state changes clearing result cache successful switch. bundled index rebuilt memory source normalization profile Unicode version differs runtime normalizer, never mixing profiles. psl_version() reports active-list identity runtime normalization identifiers needed reproduce result; psl_rules() exposes active rule table. Canonical-host deduplication: repeated host costs single punycoder normalization single C++ matcher call regardless multiplicity. non-CRAN benchmark release gate live bench/benchmark.R.
